title: Declared test command does not match project script
reasoning: The project metadata for this review says the detected test command is `npm run test`, but the actual script delegates to `bun test` while Bun is not declared as an engine, package manager, dependency, or devDependency. In a clean Node/npm environment, `npm run test` will fail if Bun is not preinstalled, which makes CI and contributor verification depend on undeclared global tooling.
reproduction: On a machine or CI image with Node/npm but without Bun, run `npm run test`; the shell cannot resolve `bun`.
recommendation: Either declare Bun as a required tool using `packageManager`/`engines` and document it in the release setup, or switch test/start scripts to tooling declared by the package. CI should install the declared package manager/runtime before invoking scripts.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: This is a toolchain declaration gap; repository tests cannot run in the affected environment because the test runner itself is the missing prerequisite.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a CI setup check or package-manager smoke job that installs from a clean checkout using the declared toolchain and runs `npm run test` and `npm run typecheck`.
minimumFixScope: Update `package.json` toolchain metadata and/or scripts so required runtimes are declared and reproducible.

history.reasoning: The original one-line quote no longer matches formatting, but the evidence still exists in package.json: scripts.test is still "bun test" at line 12, with prepack/start also invoking bun at lines 11 and 14. package.json still has no packageManager or engines field and does not declare Bun as a runtime dependency; @types/bun is only a dev type package. A bun.lock exists and docs/PRD.md says runtime/tooling is Bun, but that does not make npm-based clean setup reproducible or declare the required runtime in package metadata. Locally, `npm run test` does invoke `bun test` because Bun is installed at /Users/bjesuiter/.bun/bin/bun; the test run then failed on sandbox temp-directory EPERM, not because the finding is fixed. `npm run typecheck` passed. The issue remains open: npm scripts still depend on undeclared global Bun tooling.
commands: command -v bun; bun --version; command -v npm; npm --version

history.reasoning: The original one-line evidence no longer exists as quoted. Current package.json declares the required Bun toolchain with "packageManager": "bun@1.3.14" at line 5 and engines.bun ">=1.3.14" at lines 6-9, while scripts.test remains "bun test" at line 17. That satisfies the finding's minimum fix scope: the Bun runtime required by npm scripts is now declared in package metadata. A bun.lock is present and docs/PRD.md still documents Bun testing. Local `npm run test` did invoke Bun 1.3.14, proving the script can resolve Bun in this environment; it failed only because the read-only sandbox denies mkdtemp under the OS temp directory, not because Bun is undeclared or missing. `npm run typecheck` passed.
commands: rg -n '"(scripts|test|packageManager|engines|dependencies|devDependencies)"|bun|setup-bun|oven-sh/setup-bun|npm run test|bun test|Bun' -S package.json README.md docs .github 2>/dev/null
command -v bun; bun --version; command -v npm; npm --version
